THE NWS SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTH OF YELLVILLE, OR NEAR THE COMMUNITY OF RALPH ALONG HWY 14. ADDITIONAL DETAILS REGARDING THIS TORNADO WILL COME LATER ONCE MORE DATA IS RECIEVED.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED ALONG HIGHWAY 14 SOUTH OF YELLVILLE. FALLEN TREES WERE ON AT LEAST TWO HOMES.

UPDATED TIME AND DETAILS-TORNADO RATED EF1. THIS TORNADO FORMED ABOUT 4 MILES SSE OF THE COMMUNITY OF RALPH, OR 7 MILES SSE OF YELLVILLE IN SOUTHERN MARION CO. NEAR STATE HWY 14. SOME TREES WERE BLOWN OVER ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO A HOME ALONG COUNTY ROAD 6089. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST TOWARDS CRESTWOOD TERRACE ROAD WHERE IT DAMAGED SOME MOBILE HOMES. THIS TORNADO THEN LIFTED SHORTLY AFTERWARDS NEAR THE END OF CRESTWOOD TERRACE ROAD.
LOCAL COORDINATOR REPORTED DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES ARE BLOCKING HIGHWAY 14 SOUTH NEAR YELLVILLE. THREE PEOPLE ARE REPORTED TRAPPED IN THEIR HOMES.

*** 2 INJ *** THE NWS IN LITTLE ROCK SURVEYED A TORNADO THAT STARTED IN FAR SOUTHEAST PULASKI COUNTY AND NORTHEAST INTO SOUTHWEST LONOKE COUNTY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED ALONG A 6.4 MILE PATH, AND WAS 150 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AS AN EF-1 TORNADO AND DESTROYED 2 MOBILE HOMES ALONG ITS PATH. THERE WERE 2 MINOR INJURIES ASSOCIATED WITH ONE OF THE DESTROYED MOBILE HOMES. THERE WAS ALSO SOME TREE DAMAGE AND DOWNED POWER POLES ALONG THE TORNADO PATH.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO, WHICH STARTED JUST SOUTH OF ZINC ROAD AT BERGMAN. SURVEY IS STILL ONGOING, AND MORE INFORMATION WILL BE RELEASED ONCE THE SURVEY IS COMPLETE.

THE TORNADO FIRST HIT JUST SOUTH OF ZINC ROAD. ALONG THE PATH, THERE WERE TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED, AND POWER LINES DOWN. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND A COUPLE OF MANUFACTURED HOMES WERE BLOWN OVER OR COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A MOBILE HOME BEFORE CROSSING HIGHWAY 7 NEAR DIXON HOLLOW. IT LIFTED JUST AFTER CROSSING THE HIGHWAY. NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO, WITH WINDS OF 86-110 MPH. THE PATH WAS 6.7 MILES AND WIDTH 400 YARDS.

IT LIFTED JUST AFTER CROSSING THE HIGHWAY. NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO, WITH WINDS OF 86-110 MPH. THE PATH WAS 6.7 MILES AND WITH 400 YARDS.

**THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT** AFTER CAREFUL REVIEW OF VIDEO DATA FROM SOCIAL MEDIA, EYEWITNESS REPORTS, AND REPORTED LIGHT DAMAGE ON THE SHORE OF LAKE DARDANELLE, WEST OF RUSSELLVILLE, IT HAS BEEN DETERMINED THAT A GUSTNADO MOVED OVER THE LAKE AND CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES ALONG THE LAKE SHORE. VIDEO DETAILS SHOW A CIRCULATION OVER THE LAKE NOT CONNECTED TO A THUNDERSTORM UPDRAFT. SOME SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF OF AN OUTBUILDING AND SOME TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE SHORELINE. ESTIM
